texts.full_text: for him to be the first player to play wall of the cards in his hand. Therare also many games which have been played with penalty decks wherein there is no escape provision. In such cases when one or two players receive a large amount of penalties, they may become so discouraged that their interest and enthusiasm wanes considerably during subsequent playing periods. This can be particularly true if the player receiving an excessive number of cards has to play against more experienced opponents. It would therefore appear desirable in many card games wherein penalty decks are used for there to be some provision whereby a succeeding or following player may escape from having to accept all, part or any portion of such penalties when they occur. It is also apparent that it might well add interest and excitement if the possibility existed during play where one could avoid receiving an excessive number of cards. It would therefore appear desirable in many card games wherein penalty decks are used for there to be some provision whereby a succeeding player may escape from having to accept all, part or any portion of such penalties when they occur. Mohager) • B.Tech in Electrical Engineering, Indian Institute of Technology, Kanpur, December 1980, (specialized in computer and telecommunication engg). Research: His research includes Software Quality, Software Fault Toleran ce, Software Engineering and Software Development. Research Projects (selected): • (2017 onwards): Project: “Developing a National Software Quality Testbed”, funded by NMEICT, MHRD, India, and carried out in cooperation with IIT Guwahati, MNNIT Allahabad, IIIT, Surathkal, VNIT, Nagpur and IIT Patna. • Project: “Automating the Analysis of Code Quality”, Funded by IIT Delhi, funded for two years • Project: “Incorporating Metrics, Static Analyses and Code- Smells in the Software Code Analyser”, funded by IIT Delhi for 18 months,

texts.full_text: . There is a huge difference between the two forms. 1. You buy a song, a podcast is free. 2. You listen to songs many times, a podcast is listened to once. 3. You keep a song, you throw out a podcast. 4. I don't know about you but when I replay a song I want to start at the beginning. But if I haven't finished a podcast, I want to resume where I left off. 5. You make lists of songs, you curate them, put them next to other songs, sing them, hum them, can't get them out of your head, lisI understand the sentiment being expressed here about Zune and its limitations compared to Apple's iPod or iPhone ecosystem—especially regarding music versus podcasting. 1. It is true that with podcasts, unlike paid music downloads on services like iTunes (which was how songs were purchased at one point), they are free of charge for listeners. This has made them an accessible form of content consumption and a popular way to learn new things or stay informed without the financial commitment associated with purchasing individual tracks. 2.
